purpose: Define the repo-source line budget as a reasonable-refactor maintenance signal with advisory default behavior and explicit strict ratchet mode for maintainers and scheduled structure jobs.
machine_boundary: This contract owns line-budget thresholds, near-limit advisory thresholds, reasonable-refactor classification, and reviewed oversized-file baselines for tracked source files. It does not define domain truth, production readiness, release readiness, or quality verdict authority.

reviewed_baselines.reason: Private command registry is a stable CLI specification surface; current module refactor keeps source modules under budget while leaving this entrypoint spec as a reviewed strict-ratchet baseline.
reviewed_baselines.intended_boundary: Split by private command family when the CLI entrypoint registry becomes the active refactor target; no new private command family should grow this file without moving to a case part.

reviewed_baselines.reason: Agent Lab complete test keeps several control-plane read models in one scenario file; it is slightly above budget and reviewed as a strict-ratchet test baseline.
reviewed_baselines.intended_boundary: Split by eval, workbench, developer-mode, mechanism, export, optimize, and variant comparison scenarios during the next test-cohesion pass.

reviewed_baselines.reason: App action CLI tests cover one operator mutation boundary with shared fixtures; current source-module closure keeps it as a reviewed strict-ratchet test baseline.
reviewed_baselines.intended_boundary: Split by runtime dry-run, catalog, ScholarSkills sync, settings/release/workspace/provider, and module/scheduler/worker action groups.

reviewed_baselines.reason: App state GUI read-model test is intentionally broad and protects the default cockpit projection; it is reviewed as a strict-ratchet test baseline while source modules are kept under budget.
reviewed_baselines.intended_boundary: Split fixtures and assertions by default GUI read model, MAS activity refs, owner-delta cache guard, network lookup guard, bounded rendering, and parser validation.

reviewed_baselines.reason: Contract entry CLI tests cover loader, validator, doctor, status, session, and native helper entrypoints with shared process helpers; current line count is reviewed as a strict-ratchet baseline.
reviewed_baselines.intended_boundary: Split by contract loading, validation, doctor/status/session, and native helper lifecycle once this test lane is actively refactored.

reviewed_baselines.reason: Family runtime CLI tests share queue, lifecycle, provider, dispatch, approval, and admission fixtures; current line count is reviewed as a strict-ratchet test baseline.
reviewed_baselines.intended_boundary: Split by status/lifecycle, provider/repair, enqueue/approval/hold, attempt ledger, and admission/tick command groups.

reviewed_baselines.reason: Managed update kernel projection tests share update status, plan, apply, projection-only component, and selector fixtures; current line count is reviewed as a strict-ratchet baseline.
reviewed_baselines.intended_boundary: Split by contract/status, plan, projection-only apply guards, companion tools, workflow profile, and selector alias scenarios.

reviewed_baselines.reason: System seed manifest tests cover image seed, Docker WebUI readback, materialization, preheated payloads, canonical strategy validation, and startup-maintenance seed boundary with shared fixtures.
reviewed_baselines.intended_boundary: Split by seed apply, Docker WebUI doctor, materialization, preheated payload, strategy validation, and startup-maintenance seed boundary scenarios.

reviewed_baselines.reason: Startup-maintenance tests cover framework archive apply, package-channel artifact apply, rollback, Codex refresh, module sync, OMA install, timeout, and developer checkout scenarios with shared setup.
reviewed_baselines.intended_boundary: Split by framework artifact apply, rollback, Codex refresh, module sync, OMA managed root, health timeout, and developer checkout maintenance scenarios.

reviewed_baselines.reason: Temporal provider tests cover workflow updates, retry, activity state, replay, readiness, closeout, provider blockers, and refs-only history with shared Temporal fixtures.
reviewed_baselines.intended_boundary: Split by contract/readiness, operator updates, retry/state/replay, blocker handling, refs-only history, typed closeout, and rejection scenarios.
